description: Figure 3 presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) inverse pole figures (IPFs) showing the texture evolution of austenite along the tensile axis (TA) in a Fe-35Mn-6Al-0.1C low-carbon low-density cryogenic steel during tensile deformation at room temperature (RT) and liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT). Panel (a) displays IPFs at 0 %, 3 %, 28 %, and 44 % engineering strains at RT, while panel (b) shows IPFs at 3 %, 10 %, 28 %, and 55 % engineering strains at LNT. The color scales indicate multiples of random distribution (MRD), revealing that the initially weak austenite texture progressively intensifies with increasing strain under both conditions. At RT, the maximum MRD increases from 2.55 (0 %) to 4.81 (44 %), whereas at LNT it rises from 2.01 (3 %) to 6.68 (55 %). A strong <111>//TA fiber texture develops alongside a weaker <100>//TA component. Caption states that while the <111>//TA texture strength at RT is slightly higher than at LNT at equivalent strains, the texture intensity at maximum uniform elongation is significantly stronger at LNT due to the higher achievable tensile strain (55 % vs 44 %), supporting enhanced deformation twinning and strain hardening at cryogenic temperatures.
